title: Does SystemVerilog has generalized expression indexing?
body:
I've been reading @wsnyder 's 2010 SNUG paper today ( https://www.veripool.org/papers/Preproc_Good_Evil_SNUGBos10_paper.pdf ) inspired by it to construct a convoluted verilog macro (I'll spare you the details...)

On page 7 I ran into this quote:

_This benefit is reduced with SystemVerilog2009 which finally added the {}[] syntax. So we have two choices:_
```
`define GLOBAL_VAL 22... {`GLOBAL_VAL}[1:0]   
```
I went over the SystemVerilog 2017 standard, and couldn't find any reference to a generalized indexing operator such as {}[] .
I've even tried a small example :
```
module test;
initial
  $display("%h\n", {12345}[10:0]);
endmodule
```
And it failed miserably with a fairly recent Cadence simulator (20.03-something), as well as with Verilator :-(
What am I missing? does SystemVerilog really has this feature or not?
